title: The Sonnets XX - A womanï؟½s face with natureï؟½s own hand painted
content:
آ آ آ آ A womanï؟½s face with natureï؟½s own hand painted,
آ آ آ آ Hast thou, the master mistress of my passion;
آ آ آ آ A womanï؟½s gentle heart, but not acquainted
آ آ آ آ With shifting change, as is false womenï؟½s fashion:
آ آ آ آ An eye more bright than theirs, less false in rolling,
آ آ آ آ Gilding the object whereupon it gazeth;
آ آ آ آ A man in hue all ï؟½huesï؟½ in his controlling,
آ آ آ آ Which steals menï؟½s eyes and womenï؟½s souls amazeth.
آ آ آ آ And for a woman wert thou first created;
آ آ آ آ Till Nature, as she wrought thee, fell a-doting,
آ آ آ آ And by addition me of thee defeated,
آ آ آ آ By adding one thing to my purpose nothing.
آ آ آ آ But since she prickï؟½d thee out for womenï؟½s pleasure,
آ آ آ آ Mine be thy love and thy loveï؟½s use their treasure.
meaning: About: William Shakespeare (baptised 26 April 1564 ï؟½ 23 April 1616)[a] was an English poet and playwright, widely regarded as the greatest writer in the English language and the world's pre-eminent dramatist. He is often called England's national poet and the "Bard of Avon" (or simply "The Bard"). His surviving works consist of 38 plays, 154 sonnets, two long narrative poems, and several other poems. His plays have been translated into every major living language and are performed more often than those of any other playwright.
